tables.description: Product categories. Each category groups related products together (e.g., Beverages, Seafood, Dairy Products). Used to classify and organize the product catalog.

columns.name: category_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each product category

columns.name: category_name
columns.description: Name of the category (e.g., 'Beverages', 'Condiments', 'Seafood')

columns.name: description
columns.description: Detailed description of what products belong in this category

sample_questions: How many categories do we have?
List all product categories

tables.description: Customer companies that purchase products. Contains company details, contact information, and geographic location. Each customer can place multiple orders.

columns.name: customer_id
columns.description: Unique 5-letter identifier for each customer (e.g., 'ALFKI')

columns.name: company_name
columns.description: Official name of the customer company

columns.name: contact_name
columns.description: Name of the primary contact person at the company

columns.name: contact_title
columns.description: Job title of the contact person (e.g., 'Sales Representative', 'Owner')

columns.name: address
columns.description: Street address of the company

columns.name: city
columns.description: City where the company is located

columns.name: region
columns.description: State, province, or region (may be NULL for some countries)

columns.name: postal_code
columns.description: Postal or ZIP code

columns.name: country
columns.description: Country where the company is located (e.g., 'Germany', 'USA', 'Brazil')

columns.name: phone
columns.description: Company phone number

columns.name: fax
columns.description: Company fax number

sample_questions: How many customers are from Germany?
List all customers in the UK
Which countries have the most customers?

tables.description: Sales employees who handle orders. Contains personal details, hire dates, and reporting hierarchy. Employees are assigned to orders as the sales representative responsible.

columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each employee

columns.name: last_name
columns.description: Employee's last/family name

columns.name: first_name
columns.description: Employee's first/given name

columns.name: title
columns.description: Job title (e.g., 'Sales Representative', 'Sales Manager', 'Vice President Sales')

columns.name: birth_date
columns.description: Employee's date of birth

columns.name: hire_date
columns.description: Date the employee was hired

columns.name: address
columns.description: Employee's street address

columns.name: city
columns.description: City where the employee is located

columns.name: region
columns.description: State or region

columns.name: postal_code
columns.description: Postal code

columns.name: country
columns.description: Country where the employee is located

columns.name: phone
columns.description: Employee's phone number

columns.name: reports_to
columns.description: Employee ID of this employee's manager (self-referencing hierarchy)

sample_questions: Who are all the sales representatives?
Which employee has handled the most orders?
Show the employee reporting hierarchy

tables.description: Companies that supply/manufacture products. Contains supplier contact details and location. Each supplier can provide multiple products.

columns.name: supplier_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each supplier

columns.name: company_name
columns.description: Official name of the supplier company

columns.name: contact_name
columns.description: Name of the primary contact at the supplier

columns.name: contact_title
columns.description: Job title of the supplier contact

columns.name: address
columns.description: Street address of the supplier

columns.name: city
columns.description: City where the supplier is located

columns.name: region
columns.description: State, province, or region

columns.name: postal_code
columns.description: Postal code

columns.name: country
columns.description: Country where the supplier is located

columns.name: phone
columns.description: Supplier phone number

columns.name: fax
columns.description: Supplier fax number

columns.name: homepage
columns.description: Supplier's website URL

sample_questions: Which suppliers are from Japan?
How many products does each supplier provide?

tables.description: Shipping/delivery companies used to transport orders to customers. Each order is assigned one shipper.

columns.name: shipper_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each shipping company

columns.name: company_name
columns.description: Name of the shipping company (e.g., 'Speedy Express', 'Federal Shipping')

columns.name: phone
columns.description: Shipper's phone number

sample_questions: Which shipping companies do we use?
Which shipper handles the most orders?

tables.description: Products available for sale. Each product belongs to a category and is provided by a supplier. Contains pricing, stock levels, and discontinuation status.

columns.name: product_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each product

columns.name: product_name
columns.description: Name of the product (e.g., 'Chai', 'Chang', 'Tofu')

columns.name: supplier_id
columns.description: ID of the supplier who provides this product

columns.name: category_id
columns.description: ID of the category this product belongs to

columns.name: quantity_per_unit
columns.description: Description of the packaging (e.g., '10 boxes x 20 bags', '24 - 12 oz bottles')

columns.name: unit_price
columns.description: Current price per unit in dollars

columns.name: units_in_stock
columns.description: Number of units currently in warehouse stock

columns.name: units_on_order
columns.description: Number of units currently on order from the supplier

columns.name: reorder_level
columns.description: Minimum stock level that triggers a reorder

columns.name: discontinued
columns.description: Whether the product is discontinued (true = no longer sold)

sample_questions: What are the most expensive products?
Which products are discontinued?
Which products need to be reordered (stock below reorder level)?
How many products are in each category?

tables.description: Customer orders/purchases. Links customers to employees (sales reps) and shippers. Contains order dates, shipping details, and freight costs. Each order can have multiple line items in the order_details table.

columns.name: order_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each order

columns.name: customer_id
columns.description: ID of the customer who placed this order

columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: ID of the employee/sales rep who handled this order

columns.name: order_date
columns.description: Date the order was placed

columns.name: required_date
columns.description: Date the customer requested delivery by

columns.name: shipped_date
columns.description: Date the order was actually shipped (NULL if not yet shipped)

columns.name: shipper_id
columns.description: ID of the shipping company used for this order

columns.name: freight
columns.description: Shipping/freight cost in dollars

columns.name: ship_name
columns.description: Name of the recipient (usually the customer company name)

columns.name: ship_address
columns.description: Delivery street address

columns.name: ship_city
columns.description: Delivery city

columns.name: ship_region
columns.description: Delivery state/region

columns.name: ship_postal_code
columns.description: Delivery postal code

columns.name: ship_country
columns.description: Delivery country

sample_questions: How many orders were placed each month?
Which orders haven't been shipped yet?
What is the average freight cost per country?
Which customer placed the most orders?

tables.description: Line items within each order. Each row represents one product in an order, with its quantity, price at time of sale, and any discount applied. To calculate revenue: unit_price * quantity * (1 - discount).

columns.name: order_id
columns.description: ID of the order this line item belongs to (part of composite primary key)

columns.name: product_id
columns.description: ID of the product ordered (part of composite primary key)

columns.name: unit_price
columns.description: Price per unit at the time of the order (may differ from current product price)

columns.name: quantity
columns.description: Number of units ordered

columns.name: discount
columns.description: Discount percentage applied (0.00 = no discount, 0.10 = 10% off, 0.25 = 25% off)

sample_questions: What are the top 5 products by total revenue?
What is the total revenue across all orders?
What is the average order value?
Which products are most frequently ordered together?

relationships: products.category_id → categories.category_id (each product belongs to one category)
products.supplier_id → suppliers.supplier_id (each product comes from one supplier)
orders.customer_id → customers.customer_id (each order is placed by one customer)
orders.employee_id → employees.employee_id (each order is handled by one sales rep)
orders.shipper_id → shippers.shipper_id (each order uses one shipping company)
order_details.order_id → orders.order_id (each line item belongs to one order)
order_details.product_id → products.product_id (each line item references one product)

common_patterns.product_with_category: JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id
common_patterns.full_order_info: orders o JOIN order_details od ON o.order_id = od.order_id JOIN products p ON od.product_id = p.product_id
